response.justification: This candidate is fundamentally misaligned with the role. While they demonstrate strong software engineering fundamentals and communication skills, they lack the two critical mandatory requirements: substantial C++ experience (only ~1-2 years from 10+ years ago, mostly on Linux rather than Windows) and any financial services domain expertise. Their background is primarily full-stack web development, not systems-level capital markets software. The role requires mid-senior C++ developers with 5-10 years of Windows-based experience; this candidate's last C++ work was a decade ago at National Instruments.
strengths: Strong software craftsmanship practices including TDD, CI/CD, and infrastructure knowledge that could support development workflows
Excellent communication and mentoring abilities demonstrated through workshops, talks, and community contributions
Proven ability to learn diverse technologies and work effectively across different programming paradigms and domains
concerns: C++ experience is minimal (~1-2 years) and dated (last significant C++ work in 2015), far below the mandatory 5-10 years required
No exposure to financial services, capital markets, fixed income, or investment banking domains—critical domain knowledge is completely absent
No evidence of Windows platform development experience or SQL Server/Oracle expertise; background heavily emphasizes Linux, web technologies, and modern stacks
